purpose: Single source of truth for doc placement. lean-doc-generator MUST consult this before creating any new file in docs/. See LEAN DOCUMENTATION STANDARD: WHY/WHERE only, HOW goes in code.

docs/.naming: <NAME>.md (root-level core docs only — do NOT add new ones)

HOW.rule: NEVER goes in docs/. Belongs in code comments, SKILL.md, agent files, or types.
HOW.redirect_message: HOW content rejected — move to code comments or SKILL.md

WHERE.rule: Module map, dep graph, file layout, hub-and-spoke structure.

WHO.rule: Owners, contributors, decision rights, contact protocol.

L0_overflow.rationale: CLAUDE.md hard cap = 80 lines (governance). L0 one-liners spill into CODEMAP.md when cap reached; CLAUDE.md keeps single-line pointer.

routing_priority: 1. Match content_types HOW filter first — if HOW, REJECT (no doc created)
3. If multiple folders match, pick most specific (deepest path)
4. If zero folders match, prompt user once and append new rule to this file

on_unmapped.behavior: lean-doc-generator MUST prompt user before placing unmapped content. User answer persisted as new entry in folders or content_types.
on_unmapped.prompt_template: Content type '<tag>' has no routing rule. Suggest folder + naming pattern, then I update docs/_routing.json.

enforcement.step_description: consult docs/_routing.json before writing any file under docs/
enforcement.violation: Any new file in docs/ that bypassed this routing = scope-drift; reviewer flags BLOCKING
